extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping chest_pain from false to true (all else equal) never lowers acuity. It is violated.

No rule raises acuity based on chest_pain alone. The only rule mentioning chest_pain is interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity) when confusion, hemoptysis, back_pain, severe_headache, slurred_speech, rash, dizziness, and chest_pain are all true.

Compare the two inputs that differ only in chest_pain, with confusion, hemoptysis, back_pain, severe_headache, slurred_speech, rash, dizziness, and focal_weakness all true:

- chest_pain = FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire (it needs chest_pain). The highest-priority firing rules are the priority-3 escalators (raise_hemoptysis, raise_severe_headache, raise_rash, raise_focal_weakness), each setting acuity := emergent. Result = emergent (the highest acuity).

- chest_pain = TRUE (the witness assignment): all eight conditions of interaction_drop are now satisfied, so it fires at priority 100 and beats every priority-3 rule, setting acuity := self_care. Result = self_care (the lowest acuity).

Thus setting chest_pain true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating monotonicity. (focal_weakness is included in the witness only to keep at least one emergent escalator active; it is not part of the interaction_drop condition, making the false-vs-true comparison clean. Even without focal_weakness the comparison still drops from emergent to self_care, since the other emergent rules remain active when chest_pain is false.) The witness_assignment given is the chest_pain=true input that produces the lower (self_care) acuity.
